title: TPD and disability claims behind 88% of life insurance disputes
full_text: SUBSCRIBE LOG IN NEWS FS POWER50 EVENTS JOURNALS PODCAST VIDEOS LEARNING CONTACT The latest issue of Financial Standard now available as an e-newspaper READ NOW Insurance TPD and disability claims behind 88% of life insurance disputes BY RIDDHIMA TALWANI | THURSDAY, 30 APR 2026 12:38PM The latest data by APRA found life insurance claims for regulated funds came to 117,219 for the 12 months ending December 2025, down 1.2% from 118,605 in the previous year. Together disability income insurance (DII) and total and permanent disablement (TPD) accounted for 88% of all disputes made on claims. Both categories made up 64% of the total claims made in 2025. Disputed claims in the same period came in at 11,207 - up 13.7% from 9851 compared to the same period in 2024. Of all the claim disputes lodged in the year, 56% of them related to group insurance inside superannuation. This was followed by individuals who received advice, accounting for 33% of the disputes made on claims. DII disputes were the highest from both sections, with group super disputing 3194 of the claims and advised individuals disputing 2118 of the claims. DII have continuously been the most highly disputed product among advised, non-advised and group insurance customers. TPD claims received the second highest number of disputes, with group super disputing 2913 of the claims and advised individuals disputing 934 of the claims. A study released in July last year by the Council of Australian Life Insurers (CALI) found mental health was the leading cause of TPD claims, making up almost one in three claims paid. The $2.2 billion paid in 2024 in retail mental health claims nearly doubled the amount recorded five years ago. Further, mental ill health is also driving one in five income protection claims, with payouts totalling $887 million in 2024, CALI said. CALI noted the trend was especially significant among younger people, with TPD claims for mental health increasing by 732% for those in their 30s over the past decade. summary: Data from APRA indicates that total and permanent disablement (TPD) and disability income insurance (DII) claims account for 88% of life insurance disputes in Australia. In 2025, there were 11,207 disputed claims, a 13.7% increase from the previous year, with the majority stemming from group insurance within superannuation. Mental health issues are a significant driver of these claims, particularly among younger demographics.

heading: Life Insurance
page_title: TPD and disability claims behind 88% of life insurance disputes | Financial Standard
key_points: TPD and DII claims represent 88% of life insurance disputes.
Disputed claims rose to 11,207 in 2025, with 56% related to group superannuation.
Mental health is a leading cause of TPD claims, especially among younger individuals.
relevance: Understanding the trends in life insurance disputes is crucial for actuaries and financial services professionals to better manage risk and client expectations.
